top.name: Optimism Retro Funding
top.reason: High impact retroactive fit as the project is already shipped, running, and provides public utility.

top.name: Gitcoin Grants
top.reason: Perfect alignment with quadratic funding for open-source public goods and security tooling.

top.name: Octant
top.reason: Strong candidate for recurring community-voted public goods funding based on proven output.

top.name: Uniswap Foundation Grants
top.reason: High fit due to DeFi-specific focus on exploit warnings, TVL monitoring, and security audits.

top.name: Drips Network
top.reason: Excellent fit for continuous dependency funding given the open-core and modular nature of the engine.

top.name: Base Ecosystem / Builder Grants
top.reason: Strong alignment with 'Based' builder culture and onchain security monitoring for the Base ecosystem.

top.name: Ethereum Foundation ESP
top.reason: Strong fit for infrastructure and security research, though often favors deep protocol-level work.

top.name: DoraHacks BUIDL
top.reason: Good fit for continuous builders; the autonomous nature of the project appeals to the hacker ethos.

top.name: Arbitrum Foundation Grants
top.reason: Good fit if monitors and audit pipelines are specifically extended to support the Arbitrum ecosystem.

top.name: Polygon Community Grants
top.reason: Solid fit for dev tooling and security, provided there is a clear benefit to Polygon users.

top.name: Web3 Foundation Grants
top.reason: Moderate fit; highly technical but often prioritizes Polkadot/Substrate-specific infrastructure.

top.name: Solana Foundation Grants
top.reason: Moderate fit; requires specific integration with Solana data/RPCs to meet ecosystem-specific mandates.
